title: A Valentine for Gravity Goldfish
summary: Sophie Mouse and her friend Bearhead set out to make Valentine's Day special for an unlikely recipient: the city-destroying supervillain, Gravity Goldfish. After Sophie bakes a batch of goldfish-themed cupcakes, the duo composes an elaborate rhyming card to express their affection. Though he initially scoffs at the holiday, Gravity Goldfish is deeply moved by the unexpected kindness. Realizing that he is truly loved, the villain decides to reconsider his mischievous ways and enjoys the treats made just for him.

transcript.text: Who should we tell a story about tonight?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I think Bearhead. Bearhead and Sophie.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Once upon a time...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Ding dong Bearhead!
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Who is it? said Bearhead.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sophie!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Bearhead said, Hello Sophie. I was just thinking about rhymes. Do you ever think about rhymes, Sophie?
And no, I'm just too busy making cake.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Making cakes?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I just had to finish prepping a green sprinkled one. But I finished doing the green sprinkled one, and then I had to do a red sprinkled one, and then a pink and red sprinkled one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who are all these cakes for? said Bearhead.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They're just shaped like a bear. They've got goldfishies in them, and you know who they're for? Gravity Goldfish, to say I love you for Valentine's Day.
You're making cakes for Gravity Goldfish for Valentine's Day? Wow.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And they're shaped like fishies.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Maybe, since you're making such nice cakes for Gravity Goldfish, we could make up a rhyme for her and write it down on a card. What do you think about that? It would be a little poem.
I know a friendly friend named Isabella who is Sophie's... who's my friend, and she has a cat poem at her house.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: A cat poem? How does it go?
I don't know.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Do you want to make up a cat poem? Or a fish poem?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: A fish poem for Gravity Goldfish. All about goldfish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You should start. Once there was a fish swimming in a dish. Your turn.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then ate a snack and didn't want to have a pack.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the fish thought it was funny, but she couldn't find the honey. Then what? Your turn.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then couldn't find the denny of its baby fenny.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Couldn't find the denny of the baby fenny? And she couldn't find her money, and she couldn't find her tummy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Silly!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So what did she do next? It's your turn.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She sent someone a text.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who did she send it to? Someone wearing a shoe. Who was the person who could help? The person who would come when she yelped? And sniped and groped. The goldfish says, Please come. If you have some honey, I want some. And you know who came over there? Someone with a big head. A bear. And along came Bearhead. And then, you know what he said?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I'm tired, really, really tired.
Okay, you say a line and I'll tell a rhyme to the line. Just has to end with a word that I can rhyme to.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: I've got some slime.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And I like to rhyme!
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Yeah. Even though I'm like this, I can still make up a rhyme. Ooh, ooh, speaking a different way for starting stories today.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: And Bearhead also said, Yay yay yay! This is my favorite word game to play. All right, we gotta do a big finish about a goldfish, and maybe it could have a nice wish... something nice for our friend in this card that we will send.
Are you ready to write it?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: No, but I'm ready to bite it! Time to write our card, because making up rhymes is not very hard. And Gravity Goldfish, we love you. Guga-gaga-booboo-boo. That part was for Avery. What do you think? says Bearhead.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: It's great! Did you do the rhyme?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We did a lot of rhymes, said Bearhead. I'll write down the rhyme that we made up on the card. It's pretty long, but I think I can fit it all in. Now what else should we put on the card? said Bearhead.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I love you. And a picture of a bear and a mouse and a heart. And it says with love from Sophie Mouse and Bearhead, your friends.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, this is a nice Valentine's Day card. And should we put anything with the card?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We can put some cupcakes with it. Here, says Sophie as she pulls out her pocket, here's some cupcakes.
What flavor are these? says Bearhead.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Goldfish flavored for Gravity Goldfish to have right now. Because today is Valentine's Day. Now we can put the things in a bag and deliver them to Gravity Goldfish.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And so they brought them to Gravity Goldfish.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Hey Gravity Goldfish! says Sophie.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Yes.
We brought some goldfish cupcakes for Valentine's Day.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: But I'm an evil supervillain who destroys cities! I don't celebrate Valentine's Day! I'm Gravity Goldfish! Ha ha ha!
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well, they're just for Valentine's Day. They're just to say Happy Valentine's Day.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But nobody sends me Valentine's cards because no one likes me! But you did? Why did you do that?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because Isabella likes fish.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, does that mean she likes me?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.
And wait, what are those things next to the card? Are those cupcakes? They're not for me, are they?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: They are for you. Because they're shaped like goldfish, and they're shaped like you, Gravity Goldfish, and they've got goldfish sprinkles.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: That sounds delicious. You made goldfish flavored and goldfish shaped cupcakes just for me? My goodness. And Gravity Goldfish was overwhelmed with gratitude and happiness and said, and a card too with a drawing on it?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. It says Happy Love, Gravity Goldfish.

Wow, I'm so touched. Well, thank you. You've made a terrible day for this evil supervillain much nicer, and maybe I won't be such a mischievous supervillain after all, because now I know that someone loves me. Thank you. Nom nom nom!
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Now I think the stars and stories is done.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Okay.
Now time to do the lanterns!
transcript.speaker: Izzy

characters.name: Bearhead
characters.description: Awakened from an eon of glacial slumber, the Ice King is a massive bear with a disproportionately large head crowned in frost. Though he rules a kingdom of frozen silence, he is a master of rhymes and rhythmic riddles, emerging from his lethargy to traverse the snows and deliver heartfelt Valentine’s surprises with a whimsy that defies his biting winter chill.

characters.name: Sophie
characters.description: The resilient owner and artisan of the Eat and Greet Bakery is a vibrant pink mouse with an iconic, rounded silhouette, typically found in a pink dress and a flour-dusted blue apron. A master of alchemical confections, she kneads life into glowing pink slime alongside her loyal companion, Wenden—a slime mouse who studies the science of her color-mixing. Her delicate appearance belies a legendary endurance, famously proven when she carried her brother Winston through the freezing wilds to the gates of Snow World.

characters.name: Gravity Goldfish
characters.description: Once a soaring engine of atmospheric ruin that leveled metropolises, this sky-bound terror was transformed by a single Valentine into a silent, mended guardian. Now celebrated in song as a legendary "pretend fish," the reformed creature drifts peacefully through the clouds, keeping a gentle, protective watch over the world and its companion, Mini Mango.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: The Fish
characters.description: The subject of a rhyming poem who swims in a dish and goes on a quest for honey.

places.name: Bearhead's House
places.description: The place where Bearhead and Sophie meet to discuss their plans and practice rhymes.

places.name: Sophie's Kitchen
places.description: The location where elaborate bear-shaped cakes and goldfish-themed cupcakes are baked.

places.name: The Supervillain's Lair
places.description: The home of Gravity Goldfish where he receives a surprising and heartwarming Valentine's Day visit.
